title: Tinder Founder Shares Priceless Investing Secrets
summary: Jesse Michels interviews Justin Mateen, co-founder of Tinder and founder of JAM Fund, about his investment philosophy and what he looks for in founders. The conversation covers startup investing strategies, portfolio companies like Curative and ABL Space Systems, and briefly touches on Mateen's experience at Tinder.

acronyms.definition: The lifetime value of a user/customer to a business
The cost to acquire a customer
Media and internet company that owned Hatch Labs incubator where Tinder was developed

events.description: Justin Mateen co-founded Tinder with Sean Rad
In February 2020, Justin Mateen asked Brad Turner of Curative to consider focusing on COVID testing

key_claims.claim: JAM Fund manages $300 million in assets investing in high growth tech startups
The market size for grocery stores is $800 billion dollars a year
Curative now powers a large percentage of COVID tests in the US
Good founders are typically between 22 to 35 years old
The two main reasons companies fail are running out of money and not being able to attract the right talent
There is not a direct correlation between risk and reward in investing
Tinder was founded in 2012 by Justin Mateen and Sean Rad, mostly funded with their own money
